entries.title: Homily on Romans 31 on romans 16:9
entries.excerpt:
"Salute Urbane, my helper in the Lord."

This is a greater encomium than the other. For this even comprehends that. "And Stachys, my beloved." This again is an honor of the same kind.

entries.title: INTERPRETATION OF THE LETTER TO THE ROMANS on romans 16:9
entries.excerpt: Paul praises Urbanus more highly than Stachys, because Urbanus had helped him in his preaching and in his struggles.

entries.title: Commentary on Romans on romans 16:9
entries.excerpt: This praise is greater than the previous one; it is even the cause of it and contains it within itself: for whoever is a fellow worker is also beloved. And he crowns this one with the same.

entries.title: Commentary on Romans on romans 16:9
entries.excerpt: Then he says, greet Urbanus, our helper in Christ, i.e., in preaching the faith: a brother helped is like a strong city (Prov 18:19), and Stachys, my beloved. He mentions them together, perhaps because they lived together or were connected in some other way.
